text: 82.4g of carbs. and that is basically the whole nutrition story.

the upsides first: pleasant natural sweetener identity. people value it as a recognisable sweetener rather than as structural nutrition. easy to use in drinks and foods. simple practical upside. familiar and common. that keeps it relevant to score at all.

now the downsides: mostly concentrated sugar. that is the main reason this lands weakly in a context-driven category. heat processing weakens the case further. processed honey loses some of the argument raw honey might have made. overall payoff is weak for misc. the upside does not outweigh the sugar concentration strongly enough.
D tier. Best treated as an occasional context item, not a nutritional base.
